examples: There are times when the book suffers from excessive authorial control.
Title pages were generally prepared last in the publication process, often without full authorial assent, and in the last-minute rush to press, mistakes were frequently made.
You almost never catch a whiff of authorial self-consciousness.
The result is a kind of literary bifurcation, or splitting of the authorial self.
Dozens of libraries with big budgets have since been bidding up the stashes in authorial attics.
